name: Punjab
stopoverNote: Amritsar (ATQ) is the natural entry point and Chandigarh (IXC), 65 km from Patiala, is the easiest exit hub; Chandigarh is a Union Territory gateway rather than a Punjab stop.

diet.vegetarian: Punjab is extremely vegetarian-friendly. The Golden Temple langar is free, 24/7 and pure-vegetarian, and dhabas routinely serve makki di roti, sarson da saag, chole, dal makhani, paneer and Amritsari kulcha.
diet.vegan: Strong caution: Punjabi food is very dairy-heavy. Ghee, butter, curd, milk and paneer appear in dals, breads, sabzis, sweets and lassi. Golden Temple langar is vegetarian but often cooked or finished with ghee, so vegans should ask before eating and request oil-only cooking.
phrases: Bina ghee, makhan, doodh, ya paneer ke banaiye = please cook without ghee, butter, milk or paneer
Sirf tel mein banaiye = cook only in oil
Roti par makhan mat lagaiye = do not put butter on the roti

cities.name: Amritsar
cities.notes: Spiritual capital of Sikhism — Golden Temple, Jallianwala Bagh, Partition Museum, Gobindgarh Fort and the late-afternoon Wagah Border ceremony.

cities.name: Kapurthala
cities.notes: The 'Paris of Punjab' — French-inspired Jagatjit Palace, Moorish Mosque, Shalimar Gardens and a quiet royal-town detour en route east.

cities.name: Anandpur Sahib
cities.notes: Birthplace of the Khalsa order, anchored by Takht Sri Kesgarh Sahib and the world-class Virasat-e-Khalsa museum near the Siwalik foothills.

cities.name: Patiala
cities.notes: Royal Punjab — Qila Mubarak, Sheesh Mahal, Baradari Gardens, old-city bazaars, phulkari embroidery and classic Punjabi food culture.

itinerary.sim: Any Indian prepaid SIM (Jio or Airtel) works well across Punjab; buy at Amritsar or Chandigarh airport/store with photo ID. Coverage is strong in cities and along the highway corridor.

Amritsar.name: Amritsar
Amritsar.note: Two days is the sweet spot: one structured around the Golden Temple cluster and time-critical Wagah ceremony, one for fort culture, bazaars and the night Sukhasan ceremony.

days.note: Wagah Border dictates the day: depart central Amritsar by 2:30 PM in Oct–Mar or 3:30 PM in Apr–Sep, arrive about an hour early, and keep large bags out.

activities.name: Golden Temple (Harmandir Sahib) dawn darshan + langar
activities.tip: Go by 5–6 AM for calm light; cover your head and remove shoes. Langar is pure-veg but often uses ghee, so vegans should ask.

activities.name: Jallianwala Bagh memorial garden & gallery
activities.tip: It is a short walk from the Golden Temple; the renovated gallery is usually open 9 AM–5 PM.

activities.name: Partition Museum, Town Hall
activities.tip: Closed Mondays and some national holidays; pre-book or arrive early on weekends.

activities.name: Kesar Da Dhaba or Bharawan Da Dhaba lunch
activities.tip: Both are vegetarian institutions; expect ghee-rich dal, paratha and kulcha.

activities.name: Wagah Border Beating Retreat Ceremony
activities.cost: Free; baggage cloakroom ~₹50/bag; private cab one-way ₹600–₹1,000
activities.tip: Ceremony is late-afternoon: around 4:15 PM in Oct–Mar and 5:15 PM in Apr–Sep; seating is first-come and summer heat is severe.

activities.name: Lawrence Road / Hall Bazaar evening food walk
activities.tip: Try Amritsari kulcha, lassi, jalebi and papad; vegans should repeat no-ghee/no-butter instructions.

days.note: Keep the evening free for the Golden Temple's Sukhasan ceremony around 9:30–10:30 PM, when the Guru Granth Sahib is carried back by candlelit procession.

activities.name: Gobindgarh Fort — Punjabi cultural experience
activities.tip: Open roughly 10 AM–10 PM; the 7D Sher-e-Punjab show and live Bhangra/Gatka are the highlights.

activities.name: Durgiana Temple
activities.tip: A silver-doored Hindu temple complex on a tank, architecturally echoing the Golden Temple.

activities.name: Hall Bazaar — phulkari, juttis, kada bangles and papad
activities.tip: Bargain politely and keep small cash for shops.

activities.name: Maharaja Ranjit Singh Panorama / Museum
activities.tip: Near Ram Bagh Gardens; useful context on the Sikh Empire.

activities.name: Golden Temple evening Sukhasan ceremony
activities.tip: Join the marble parikrama respectfully; the complex is open 24h and reflections are beautiful at night.

hotels.name: Budget hostel near the old city

Kapurthala.name: Kapurthala
Kapurthala.note: A compact royal detour: 5–6 hours covers the palace exterior, Moorish Mosque, gardens and lunch before continuing to Anandpur Sahib.

days.note: Jagatjit Palace is now Sainik School Kapurthala, so interior access requires advance permission; exterior and gardens are the safer plan.

activities.name: Jagatjit Palace exterior & gardens
activities.tip: Call Sainik School Kapurthala (+91-1822-232210) before arrival for interior access; carry photo ID.

activities.name: Moorish Mosque (Mubarak Mosque)
activities.tip: A rare Punjab replica of Moroccan/Andalusian mosque architecture; visit outside prayer times.

activities.name: Shalimar Gardens
activities.tip: Quiet Mughal-style garden, best in softer morning light.

activities.name: Panch Mandir
activities.tip: A small five-temple complex with an unusual architectural mix.

activities.name: Kanjli Wetland
activities.tip: Ramsar-listed wetland about 8 km from town; best Oct–Feb for migratory birds.

hotels.name: Amritsar or Anandpur Sahib base instead

meals: Clean dhaba near Kapurthala bus stand
Tea and sweets near the market

Anandpur Sahib.name: Anandpur Sahib
Anandpur Sahib.note: One full day covers the Takht, Virasat-e-Khalsa and nearby historic gurdwaras; add time if visiting during Hola Mohalla or adding Naina Devi.

days.note: Virasat-e-Khalsa is closed on Mondays and for maintenance windows around 24–31 January and 24–31 July, so plan this city Tue–Sun.

activities.name: Takht Sri Kesgarh Sahib Gurdwara
activities.tip: One of Sikhism's five Takhts and the site where Guru Gobind Singh founded the Khalsa in 1699; head covering mandatory and langar is available.

activities.name: Virasat-e-Khalsa Museum
activities.cost: Free basic entry; optional full-day pass ~₹100
activities.tip: Tue–Sat about 10 AM–4:30 PM, Sun 8 AM–6 PM; photography is usually restricted inside.

activities.name: Qila Anandgarh Fort ruins
activities.tip: Short hillock walk tied to Guru Gobind Singh's defensive forts.

activities.name: Gurudwara Anandgarh Sahib + Gurudwara Fatehgarh Sahib
activities.tip: Historic gurdwaras dot the small town and make a peaceful evening circuit.

activities.name: Naina Devi Temple side trip
activities.tip: 28 km away across the Himachal border; best if you have an early start or a second night.

Patiala.name: Patiala
Patiala.note: Two easy days cover the royal museums and bazaars plus a lighter shrine-and-departure morning toward Chandigarh.

days.note: Qila Mubarak, Sheesh Mahal and Baradari Gardens sit within a short auto radius; confirm Monday closures and restoration status locally.

activities.name: Qila Mubarak Complex — fort, Raan Baas and Darbar Hall
activities.tip: Some sections may be under restoration; ask at the gate which galleries are open.

activities.name: Sheesh Mahal museum and mirror-work palace
activities.tip: Usually Tue–Sun 10 AM–5 PM; photography surcharge may apply.

activities.name: Baradari Gardens
activities.tip: A calm 12-arch garden pavilion, ideal after the museums.

activities.name: Moti Bagh Palace / NIS exterior view
activities.tip: Now the National Institute of Sports; civilian campus access is limited, so treat it as exterior-only unless pre-arranged.

activities.name: Adalat Bazaar + Sirhindi Gate shopping walk
activities.tip: Best for phulkari embroidery, Punjabi juttis, Patiala salwar fabric and sweets.

days.note: A lighter day designed for an onward transfer to Chandigarh, 65 km away, or to rail/bus connections via Rajpura and Ambala.

activities.name: Gurdwara Dukhniwaran Sahib
activities.tip: Peaceful sarovar complex associated with Guru Tegh Bahadur; langar is available.

activities.name: Kali Mata Temple, Patiala
activities.tip: Important local Hindu shrine close to the centre.

activities.name: Old Patiala City heritage walk
activities.tip: Morning is best for old havelis, daily markets and cooler lanes around Qila Mubarak.

activities.name: Patiala sweets and brunch stop
activities.tip: Try pinni, til revdi and fresh makkhan if you eat dairy.

activities.name: Depart for Chandigarh gateway
activities.tip: Chandigarh airport and railway station are the cleanest exit points for Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru and Shimla links.

hotels.name: Chandigarh airport hotel for early flights

connections.method: Taxi via Phagwara/Nawanshahr or bus with a change
Taxi recommended; bus/train require changes via Rupnagar/Chandigarh/Rajpura
PRTC bus or taxi to exit hub

extras: Golden Temple and its langar are free, 24h and pure-vegetarian; carry a head covering or use the free scarves at the entrance.
Wagah Border is a late-afternoon, timing-critical ceremony: 4:15 PM in Oct–Mar and 5:15 PM in Apr–Sep; leave central Amritsar 1.5–2h before ceremony and avoid large bags.
Virasat-e-Khalsa and Partition Museum are closed on Mondays; build the route so Anandpur Sahib falls Tue–Sun.
Punjabi food is very dairy-heavy — vegans must repeatedly ask for no ghee, butter, curd, milk or paneer, including at langar and dhabas.
Hola Mohalla at Anandpur Sahib (4–6 Mar 2026) is extraordinary but very crowded; book accommodation months ahead or avoid those dates.
Quoted prices are verified or best-available 2025/26 India prices as of Jul 2026; smaller palace/museum fees can change after restoration or local notices.
